input.command:
python3 - << 'PY'
import glob,re
# read problems.txt
p=' _outputs/problems.txt'
with open('_outputs/problems.txt','r',encoding='utf-8') as f:
    txt=f.read()
entries = txt.split('\n---\n')
outs = {}
for of in glob.glob('_outputs/euler_solutions/*.out'):
    lines = open(of,'r',encoding='utf-8').read().splitlines()
    if not lines: continue
    m=re.match(r'ID:\s*(\d+)', lines[0])
    if not m: continue
    id=m.group(1)
    ans_line = None
    note = ''
    t = ''
    for L in lines[1:]:
        if L.startswith('Answer:'):
            ans_line = L[len('Answer:'):].strip()
        if L.startswith('Note:'):
            note = L[len('Note:'):].strip()
        if L.startswith('Time:'):
            t = L[len('Time:'):].strip()
    outs[id] = (ans_line,note,t)
# update entries
new_entries = []
for e in entries:
    for id,(a,note,t) in outs.items():
        if f'ID: {id}\n' in e or e.startswith(f'ID: {id}'):
            # replace Answer line
            e = re.sub(r'Answer:.*','Answer: %s (time: %s)\nNote: %s' % (a,t,note), e)
    new_entries.append(e)
newtxt = '\n---\n'.join(new_entries)
open('_outputs/problems.txt','w',encoding='utf-8').write(newtxt)
print('UPDATED')
PY
